prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response:
Verified Purchase  
After 3 a.m. checkout I was auto-enrolled in a compulsory “Waking Up” trial; delivery was instant but packaging was limited to a vague sense of dread. Unboxing occurred in absolute darkness with no included manual—only a slowly brightening room and an urgent bladder notification. Firmware (cognitive function) took 12–15 minutes to load, during which every joint produced a concerning creak compatible with previous-model dreams. There is no snooze slider, and attempts to return to sender were ignored; customer support (coffee) had to be sourced separately. Two stars: it functions exactly as advertised, but the vendor refuses to disclose whether daily subscription renewals are mandatory for life.
